node.html:
black pepper beef soup attempt #1

adapted andy cook's yukgaejang but skipped the gochugaru/garlic/sesame oil and fernbrake, and added 1/4-1/2 cup black peppercorns.

i tasted it and I think it needs an additional ingredient and accent flavor to round it out. Also add herbs next time. filter the peppercorns after the broth is done.

some suggested pairings i found for next time:

  • garlic
  • basil
  • ginger
  • parsley
  • thyme
  • peas
  • scallions
  • spinach
  • vegetables
  • radishes maybe too as garnish

completed 2026-03-28

i used a pose app to make a reference for this, and then spent perhaps too long trying to get everything just right. i probably just need to practice the body's proportions more

also screw that flower on her jacket, that was really annoying to do

brass pen

june 2025

i somewhat spontaneously bought a bunch of little brass and tin parts from blick and then i figured that a g7 ink well would probably fit in them.'

so i made an arrangement and started supergluing things together and it worked!

not pictured, but later on for accent i put some leftover veneer on part of the barrel and the end cap.

funny thing is i lost this recently so maybe it shall come back into my life at some later serendipitous date.

coasters

fall 2025

i used some leftover wood and veneer for these because i wanted to get some practice doing that. they came out ok. i was not very careful to sand precisely to final dimensions so they are not super accurate.

however, the friend who received them reports that they do in fact work so that's a win!

dining chair cushions

february 2026

i got some nice tall back chairs to go with the dining table but the cushions were kind of an ugly gray. so i got some cool fabric online and stapled it on!

it was not super hard although the cushions are not precisely square so i had to be a little careful to avoid getting stuff bunched up. but i managed to make it work!

i did get bored halfway through but i had enough time to convince myself that i would be better off finishing the project up and not having to think about it anymore

none of us are free until all of us are free

just a list of some places that i send money to, if anyone's interested. i am a big fan of setting up recurring donations because it lets me do ongoing good while only have to make a few decisions one time.

  • equal justice initiative
  • partners in health
  • eloheh
  • massachusetts immigraition and refugee coalition
  • brookline food pantry
  • wikimedia foundation
  • donorschoose
  • beyond bond and legal defense fund
  • rainn
  • american foundation for suicide prevention
  • pine street inn
  • blender foundation

i also think stuff like gdq is super cool and i encourage anyone reading this into video games to check that out with checkbook in hand

i do not want to be a dragon

dyrlund restoration

february 2026

i bought a fancy table*! the tradeoff was that the top had been (poorly) veneered at some point, so i wanted to find a solution for that.

the sellers (who were very nice and delivered to my house / showed me how to assemble) suggested i just flip the top and use the bottom side as the new table top.

i thought about stripping the aftermarket veneer off but getting a good quality veneering on a surface this big is something i don't want to try for the first time on such a nice piece of furniture. so i did as they suggested.

this meant i just needed to flip the table top and then drill holes through so that i could screw the brackets in on the reverse side. i ended up using my pin vice because i was impatient and it worked.

after that there were the existing screw holes and one central hole on the (formerly) bottom side of the table top so i wanted to fill them. i tried some leftover mother-of-pearl i had lying around but that looked bad so instead i got some dowels off of amazon and carved them down until they fit snug. then i carved them (carefully) flush with the table top and i was done!

(did I say carefully? i put several scrapes in my thumbs. safety should be first if you try this at home)

*it is called a dyrlund lotus table i think. most of the ones online are much more expensive than mine was

gochujang salad dressing

a result of one of my culinary experiments but this one came together just fine on the first try so i am not going to bother to list it that way.

ingredients

  • ~1 part rice vinegar
  • ~1 part sesame oil
  • gochugaru to taste
  • gochujang to taste
  • spoonful of soy sauce (maybe ~1/4 part or something)

instructions

combine all ingredients. throw on leafy greens like spinach, scallions, etc.

headboard

sometime in 2025

i wanted to get one of those mortise-and-tenon joint bedframes but the cheap one did not come with a headboard. so i made my own!

i used an ikea desk (don't remember what it is called but about as wide as the frame), and bought upholstery batting, a mattress topper, and a linen-look shower curtain.

i stapled the mattress topper to the desk top (i also bought a staple gun for this!) and then stapled the batting over it, and then finally the shower curtain.

this ended up ok, but the shower curtain is not a great texture or match for my sheets (it's about the same color) so i want to change it. i will probably buy real upholstery fabric this time because i can do with just 2 yards i think maybe three.

completed 2025-01-07

this seemed like an appropriate setting. i tried to be a little better at simplifying the background here because i always underestimate the amount of work it is to add random stuff in high detail

hummus

surely this recipe is not original but it is at least nice for myself to remember

ingredients

  • 1 can chickpeas
    • you can wash and shell them if you are feeling extra but it is optional and a lot of work
  • tahini
  • olive oil
  • lemon juice
  • 1-2 cloves garlic
  • salt (carthage)

instructions

rinse and maybe shell the chickpeas (up to thou, who knoweth thine level of commitment greater than this humble author).

put ingredients into food processor and process them as this is its sacred purpose.

adjust quantities of other stuff based on taste.

i do not want to be a dragon

2026-03-22

i do not want to be a dragon

i do not want to strive towards
obtainment of a meagre hoard
till riches, greed and cruelty be
the only things that i can see

i do not want to grow thick skin
lest all i am is trapped within
and all without is only seen
as what it could partake from me
and mine

and e'er grows mine hoarded gold, yet
eyes and heart encrust with cold
as home turns lair, and creeps, befalls
my eyes now ending on its walls

no more to see the world engreened,
all its folk laugh in its streams,
trifles, whiskeys, wines, and cheese
shared by all who have their need

i do not want to be a dragon

todo

  • add more art|i am a little scared to do this
  • links and stuff i suppose

linen pillowcase(s) (and napkins)

thanksgiving 2025

i wanted to learn some sewing so i got linen as a pillowcase for my mini buckwheat pillow because the one that came with it was not great.

sewing turns out to not be terribly hard if you are willing to accept imperfection!

also pictured are some napkins i made with left overs. next time i need to be a little fancier about these though

mage

completed 2025-03-29

this one came together a little easier than i expected, but i think that's because i got to skip the hard parts like the hands and face. and also i guess i didn't really need to color much

memento mori

completed 2025-09-02

a commission for a coworker. i made one of these spontaneously because turns out you can just buy watch parts from ebay! and then they saw it and wanted one

i think this one came out much better and i credit that to the financial incentive and i guess the fact that i had in fact done one before

nerf gjallarhorn repaint

september 2024

nerf made a gjallarhorn (yay) but they also may it orange (nay). so i repainted it with some acrylic paints i got from blick

i did tape some stuff at first but i did not take it apart. that may have been a faster way but i didn't want to risk losing something

after the base coats i did a little wash on the silver section using diluted black and then a heavy set of washes on the gold using this sepia ink i got on sale from blick like two years before.

mini infinity pipe

september 2024

i wanted to play around with infinity mirrors so i got some plastic rolls of half-mirror and mirror to play with.

i used a toilet paper tube which i cut roughly in half, then some acrylic i had (don't remember where that was from).

to add some impressions of "pipe" i wrapped some aluminum wire around the inside and outside and put pencil parts in the inside.

then i jammed a hole in the middle for a micro LED that i got for a semi-successful silmarils project.

possum sync

completed 2025-12-25

possums are commonly know to perform all manner of occult ritual and devious wizardry

pajama shirt

march 2026

i made a set of pajamas over thanksgiving 2025 at my parents' but i improvised the shirt and it was kinda bad.

so i bought a hippari pattern because that looked like it would be an interesting shirt to use as pajamas.

i got blue linen and also i had bought a sewing machine for my new apartment because why would i want to not have a tool lying around?

i made a muslin that came out decent so i just did the real one as soon as the blue arrived. after i was done i figured the fancy leaf stitch would look good around the edges with yellow thread so i did that on a slower speed.

todo: picture of it being worn if i am brave enough for that

march 2026

made this using some leftover wood as a birthday gift for a friend. the main body is a square walnut dowel that i cut and sanded more or less to a cube, and the pips are a cherry dowel that i carved to the right thickness and jammed in with superglue.

i had some tear out while drilling the holes on this side. fortunately i was able to recover by mixing some superglue and walnut sawdust and using that to fill the gap back up after i had glued each sides' pips in.

unfortunately, said tear out also meant that the drill bit slipped into my thumb, which did make a moderate cut. hence the tape used as bandage:

i finished with some wax that i had leftover as well. turned out cool! i did test it with 100 rolls to see how fair it was and it was actually not too bad - 3s and 5s came in at roughly 13% which supposedly was just within the bounds of "fair" according to the website that i did this on.
